accept: act show <id> in human/text mode renders a 'commits:' section listing work commits whose subject matches '(act-<short>)' (most-recent-first, capped at N — pick a sensible default).
act show <id> --json includes a 'commits' array with {sha, subject, author_date} entries; empty array when no matches.
Implementation calls git log --all --grep=... at show time; no caching, no index changes.
act show on an issue with zero work commits (e.g. a tracking issue closed without code) renders no 'commits:' section in text mode and an empty array in JSON mode (not an error).
payload.description:
Conversation 2026-05-10 (act-a659 design discussion): the marker pattern (`act-op: (act-XXXX) <op>` for op commits and `(act-XXXX)` in work commits) already gives bidirectional traceability via `git log --grep=act-XXXX`. What's missing is surface — agents have to drop into git log to see which work commits are attributed to a given issue. The op-side is covered by `act log <id>`; the gap is specifically work commits.

Becomes more valuable now that act-a659 lands a design where the close op rides the work commit (not a separate act-op commit) — the work commit IS the close commit, and the agent reading `act show` after the fact has no easy way to find it without separate git incantations.

Proposal: `act show <id>` runs `git log --all --grep="(act-<short>)" --pretty=format:%h %s` and renders the matching commits as a "commits:" section in the human output. Probably top N with a hint to use `--all-commits` or similar for the full set. JSON output gets a `commits: [{sha, subject, author_date}]` field so MCP consumers can act on it.

Read-side only — no new write commits, no extra git operations during the work loop. Cost is one git invocation at show-time which is negligible.

Out of scope:
- Showing diffs (`git show <sha>` is one command away).
- Cross-referencing commits to specific ops (the (act-XXXX) marker doesn't say which op a work commit subsumed, just that it touches the issue).
- A separate `act commits <id>` subcommand — fold into show.
payload.title: act show should display work commits attributed to the issue
